preamble: The shift from the voiced velar stop [ɡ] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a process of lenition and vocalization. This transformation typically involves the weakening of the velar closure into a fricative [ɣ] before fully vocalizing into a glide, often encouraged by neighboring back vowels.

examples.note: Historical lenition of the voiced velar fricative (from earlier stop) into a labiovelar glide.
Rapid or informal speech reduction of the intervocalic velar into a glide-like approximant.

sources: Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
